english: deformed, disfigured, having lost his or her former beauty Cs.
diminished Cs.; v. ˳gyág-pa ('gyag pa).

pisé, earth or clay stamped into moulds, and frequently used as building-material in Sp., Ld., and other parts of Tibet; gyaṅ-sgróm (gyang sgrom) pisé-mould; gyaṅ-skór (gyang skor) pisé-wall round an estate or village Glr.; gyaṅ-ra (gyang ra) cattle-yard constructed of pisé; gyaṅ-tse (gyang tse) terrace wall of pisé Ld.; gyaṅ-rím (gyang rim) one layer of pisé, i.e. as much as is stamped in at a time, about one ell in height; this frequently serves for a measure of the depth of the snow Mil.; gyaṅ-rís (gyang ris) fresco or wall-painting.

also gyád-pa (gyad pa), 1. a champion, a man of great physical strength, an athlete, frq.; da-duṅ (da dung) gyád-gyi (gyad gyi) tsal (tsal) gran (gran) let us try once more our strength in fighting Mil.; gyád-rdo (gyad rdo) giant-stone, i.e. a stone which only a giant is able to lift Mil. -- 2. n. of a people Tar. 11, 10.
Med., gyan-rgyui (gyan rgyu'i) bu-ga (bu ga), gyan-rgyui (gyan rgyu'i) mtu? (mtu?)

a shelter, a grotto large and wide, but not deep (cf. skyibs (skyibs)), brág-gyam (brag gyam) a shelter under a rock; gád-gyam (gad gyam) a grotto beneath a conglomerate rock; poṅ-gyám (pong gyam) (for p'a-boṅ-gyam (pha bong gyam)) a shelter under a beetling rock: gyám-bu (gyam bu) a little cover or shelter Cs.
prob. = gyod-k'a (gyod kha), god-pa (god pa) loss, damage.
for gyi (gyi), after n (n), m (m), r (r), l (l); v. kyi (kyi).
1. bad, coarse, mean, poor, miserable, of food, clothes etc.; gyí-na (gyi na) ts'ó-ba (tsho ba) a miserable, starving life Pth. -- 2. unsteady, fickle Schr.
Glr. n. of an excellent breed of horses.
caoutchouc, India rubber, gyig-šiṅ (gyig shing), gyig-sdoṅ (gyig sdong) caoutchouc-tree Sik.
n. of a deity Pth., perh. = kiṅ-káṅ (king kang).
W. gently sloping, gradually descending or subsiding.
v. kyin (kyin)
amalgam; gyim-bág-gis (gyim bag gis) byúg-pa (byug pa) to gild in the fire Schr.
1. inst. of kyis (kyis), after liquid letters. -- 2. v. bgyid-pa (bgyid pa).
Cs. = gya-gyú (gya gyu); cf. also sgyu (sgyu). -- gyú-ba (gyu ba) v. ˳gyú-ba ('gyu ba).
v. gyoṅ-ro (gyong ro); gyur (gyur) v. ˳gyúr-ba ('gyur ba).
crookedness, curve; hunch, hump, crookback, crooked; gyé-gu-čan (gye gu can) of a camel, gyé-gur (gye gur) ˳dúg-pa ('dug pa) being crooked, of trees, opp. to draṅ-po (drang po), Stg.
n. of a Bonpo idol (?) Mil.
v. gyaṅ (gyang).
v. ˳gyéd-pa ('gyed pa).

(opp. to t'ur (thur)) up, upward, up-hill, mostly followed by du (du) or la (la), gyén-du (gyen du) ˳dzég-pa ('dzeg pa) to mount up, to ascend; gyén-du (gyen du) rdzé-ba (rdze ba) to turn up, to cock (a hat or cap); above, on the surface, gyén-du (gyen du) lús-pa (lus pa) to keep above (water) Glr. *gyen-la (gyen la) daṅ-po* (dang po) W. perpendicular, vertical; gyen-č'ád (gyen chad) (opp. to man-č'ád (man chad)) the upper part of a country, pú-rig (pu rig) gyen-č'ád (gyen chad) Upper Purig, Ld.-Glr. Schl. 26, b. also sbst.: gyen (gyen) rzár-po (rzar po) a steep ascent C.
v. dgyér-ba (dgyer ba).
v. ˳gyé-ba ('gye ba).

1. gravel, grit Dzl., Stg. -- 2. potsherd Cs.; gyo-dúm (gyo dum) id. -- 3. tile, brick Sch.; gyo-mgó (gyo mgo) id.; clay-vessel. In an allegorical comparison of the body with a house, the hair of the head is said to be like a p'ó-gyo (pho gyo) mo-gyói (mo gyo'i) rdza (rdza) Med.? gyo-rtsi (gyo rtsi) Wdn.?
curved, crooked Cs.; gyóg-po (gyog po) left-handed, awkward Sch.
C. pronounced *g'yog (g'yog)*, *g'yo (g'yo)*, for sgyogs (sgyogs) cannon, large gun.
want, need, indigence, lto-gós-kyi (lto gos kyi) gyoṅ (gyong) t'ég-pa (theg pa) to be able to endure want of food and clothes Mil.; k'ur-ba (khur ba) to be
